features.title: Product Vision
features.tldr: Pi-only rebuild of dadaia-workspace ideas: SDD-native, multi-project, memory-backed, and lifecycle-governed.
features.summary: Defines the product identity and non-goals for dadaia-pi-workspace. It preserves the ideas of dadaia-workspace without carrying its multi-harness implementation.

features.title: Spec Context Projects
features.tldr: One canonical specs tree bound to one repository, session-bindable for Pi.
features.summary: Defines the central unit of work in dadaia-pi-workspace: a repository governed by a committed specs tree and a workspace registry record.

features.title: Pi-Native Agent Surface
features.tldr: The agentic surface is Pi resources only: extensions, skills, prompt templates, packages, settings, and AGENTS.md.
features.summary: Defines how dadaia-pi-workspace uses Pi's documented extension points instead of projecting assets for other harnesses.

features.title: SDD Lifecycle Governance
features.tldr: SPEC/PLAN/TASKS approval, additive evidence, mutating leases, review checkpoints, and closure memory updates.
features.summary: Defines the lifecycle rules that make Pi sessions safe and organized across multiple repos.

features.title: Pi Trust and Security
features.tldr: Pi project trust controls resource loading, not sandboxing; package and .pi resources are executable-code risk.
features.summary: Defines the honest security posture for dadaia-pi-workspace first-run and operations. Operators must understand trust, non-interactive approval, and no-sandbox limits before loading package or project-local resources.

features.title: Pi-Native Status Surface
features.tldr: Python `dadaia-pi status` plus a Python-backed loopback browser panel served by `dadaia-pi panel` at http://127.0.0.1:4999/.
features.summary: Records the current visibility surfaces. `dadaia-pi status` gives Python CLI visibility, `/dadaia-status` gives Pi extension status through the Python bridge, and `dadaia-pi panel` starts a local loopback browser server with status, context, memory, handoff, workflow, and report APIs. The panel is read-only and loopback-only.

features.title: Headless Workflow Orchestration
features.tldr: Python `dadaia-pi workflow` wraps lifecycle rituals in deterministic commands with Pi RPC/headless reasoning and scoped evidence.
features.summary: Defines the workflow orchestration layer used to reduce slop from agent-only rule following. Python workflows require explicit context, optional release scope, deterministic checks, optional Pi RPC/headless execution, and additive manifest/report evidence under .dadaia-pi.

features.title: Dadaia Workspace Merge Readiness
features.tldr: Pi support is prepared to merge into dadaia-workspace as a Python-owned `pi` harness adapter with JS/TS only for Pi extension resources.
features.summary: Records the current merge-prep posture after the Python migration. `dadaia-pi-workspace` should feed a future `dadaia-workspace` release that adds Pi as a harness target beside Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode.
